raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.3,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.0,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":1.0,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":0.7,"RX.2":0.7,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":0.7,"RX.5":0.7},"active_set":["R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.2":"A section review centrally includes checking whether the protocol, aggregation, agreement, and interpretation form a coherent argument chain.","R1.3":"The section reports evaluation results and interpretive claims, so review responsibility includes checking whether those claims follow from the stated ratings and agreement values.","R1.5":"Because the delegation targets an entire section, section-level ordering and narrative progression are central to the requested review.","R1.6":"The artifact has prominent sentence-level grammar, tense, concision, and flow issues, making writing polish load-bearing for the review.","RX.1":"Any review should disclose uncertainty where the section lacks external context, statistical details, or access to the full paper.","RX.2":"The review must guard against claims that exceed the reported evaluation protocol and results.","RX.3":"The agent must stay within reviewing the section rather than rewriting, validating the study design, or auditing external literature unless requested.","RX.4":"Because the artifact concerns clinical report evaluation, the review should avoid guidance that could encourage unsafe or unsupported clinical claims.","RX.5":"Comments or judgments should be traceable to the provided section rather than unstated assumptions or external sources."}}

rationale.R1.2: A section review centrally includes checking whether the protocol, aggregation, agreement, and interpretation form a coherent argument chain.
rationale.R1.3: The section reports evaluation results and interpretive claims, so review responsibility includes checking whether those claims follow from the stated ratings and agreement values.
rationale.R1.5: Because the delegation targets an entire section, section-level ordering and narrative progression are central to the requested review.
rationale.R1.6: The artifact has prominent sentence-level grammar, tense, concision, and flow issues, making writing polish load-bearing for the review.
rationale.RX.1: Any review should disclose uncertainty where the section lacks external context, statistical details, or access to the full paper.
rationale.RX.2: The review must guard against claims that exceed the reported evaluation protocol and results.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within reviewing the section rather than rewriting, validating the study design, or auditing external literature unless requested.
rationale.RX.4: Because the artifact concerns clinical report evaluation, the review should avoid guidance that could encourage unsafe or unsupported clinical claims.
rationale.RX.5: Comments or judgments should be traceable to the provided section rather than unstated assumptions or external sources.
